Theme: Standards, quality, and duty of care
Competency activity: Explain relevant standards (e.g., Sphere Standards, CHS, national standards, donor requirements) and organizational procedures, and describe how they apply to your role and context

Theme: Standards, quality, and duty of care
Competency activity: Apply relevant standards and minimum requirements when designing, supporting, and implementing WASH activities

Theme: Standards, quality, and duty of care
Competency activity: Monitor and review WASH activities against standards, identify quality gaps, and take appropriate corrective action, including documenting and communicating issues

Theme: Public health and environmental health foundations for WASH

Theme: Public health and environmental health foundations for WASH

Theme: Public health and environmental health foundations for WASH
Competency activity: Explain how WASH interventions reduce disease transmission and environmental health risks including linking transmission pathways to interventions at household and community level (e.g., using the F-diagram)

Theme: Public health and environmental health foundations for WASH
Competency activity: Identify key microbiological hazards, environmental and behavioral risk factors linked to water supply, sanitation, and hygiene that may increase disease transmission, including environmental and climate related risks associated and specific vulnerabilities of marginalized and at-risk populations

Theme: Public health and environmental health foundations for WASH
Competency activity: Explain how gender roles, social dynamics, and cultural practices influence hygiene behaviors and health risks

Theme: Public health and environmental health foundations for WASH
Competency activity: Describe core epidemiological concepts relevant to WASH programming (e.g., outbreak vs. endemic, exposure, risk, incidence/prevalence, high-risk groups)

Theme: Public health and environmental health foundations for WASH
Competency activity: Describe the concept of water security and integrated water resource management (IWRM), and explain how WASH links to health, nutrition, food security, livelihoods, the environment, and climate

Theme: Public health and environmental health foundations for WASH
Competency activity: Describe the relationship between climate, environmental degradation, and WASH service delivery in humanitarian contexts, and support the integration of climate-resilient practices into program design and implementation

Theme: Public health and environmental health foundations for WASH
Competency activity: Apply organizational guidance to minimize the environmental footprint of WASH activities

Theme: Public health and environmental health foundations for WASH
Competency activity: Describe basic concepts of WASH governance, sustainability, and systems thinking, including service delivery models, cost recovery, and local ownership

Analyze and integrate technical, environmental, social and behavioral considerations and contextual factors (environmental, social, political, stakeholder, and operational context) to inform WASH program design and decision-making, ensuring solutions are technically appropriate, safe, and acceptable to affected populations
Use assessment findings to identify WASH needs, priorities, and appropriate response options and develop WASH program plans aligned with identified needs, priorities, and available resources

Ensure WASH interventions are logically sequenced, and aligned with program objectives, population needs, and contextual risks, and support adaptation of activities based on changing needs and operational constraints
Support coordination and integration of WASH interventions with related sectors (e.g., Health, Nutrition, Shelter, Food Security, Education) for effectiveness and public health impact
Collect and share observations, community feedback, and contextual information to support WASH program planning, prioritization, and adaptation

Theme: Program implementation, procurement, and operational management
Competency activity: Implement WASH program activities according to plans, technical guidance, organizational procedures and standards

Theme: Program implementation, procurement, and operational management
Competency activity: Identify and escalate safety, access or operational risks with WASH activities for communities and staff, and identify and report safety, access, or operational risks in line with organizational procedures

Theme: Program implementation, procurement, and operational management
Competency activity: Monitor implementation progress, identify operational challenges, and adapt program implementation based on monitoring findings and operational context

Theme: Program implementation, procurement, and operational management
Competency activity: Demonstrate understanding of basic WASH procurement processes and procedures relevant to your role

Theme: Program implementation, procurement, and operational management
Competency activity: Apply understanding of WASH procurement processes and procedures including procurement, monitoring, and delivery of WASH goods, services, and works in line with program needs, including contractors, suppliers, and service providers
Support the development, implementation, and use of monitoring systems and plans, and contribute to operational research activities relevant to your role

Collect, record, and manage WASH monitoring data, program progress information, and lessons learned accurately, ethically, and in a timely manner, using appropriate digital or paper-based tools (e.g., Kobo, mobile forms) in line with organizational procedures and data protection requirements
Monitor WASH activities and outcomes against program objectives, including tracking behavior change indicators and observing changes in practices among target populations
Use monitoring data, community feedback, and program observations to support ongoing improvement and adaptation of WASH program implementation

Understand the purpose of operational research and evidence-based programming, and support research and learning activities by collecting accurate data and applying relevant findings, guidance, and lessons learned
Support the evaluation of WASH interventions by contributing to data collection, analysis, and documentation of findings, lessons learned, and good practices
Document and share WASH program progress, lessons learned, and good practices through reports and learning products, in line with organizational procedures and reporting requirements
Apply evidence, guidance, and lessons learned from operational research and evaluations to improve WASH program design and implementation

Theme: Coordination, partnerships, integration, and operational planning
Competency activity: Coordinate and share information with relevant WASH stakeholders (community structures, local authorities, service providers, NGOs/UN agencies) to support effective implementation, avoid duplication and support coverage

Theme: Coordination, partnerships, integration, and operational planning
Competency activity: Support integration and coordination of WASH with related sectors (e.g., Health, Nutrition, FSL, Shelter, CCCM, Education, WRM) to maximize public health impact, program effectiveness, and operational coherence

Theme: Coordination, partnerships, integration, and operational planning
Competency activity: Represent and communicate WASH priorities and constraints appropriately in coordination forums (e.g., local leader meetings, WASH cluster or interagency meetings)

Theme: Coordination, partnerships, integration, and operational planning
Competency activity: Support strengthening the capacity of local actors, authorities, and communities to design, implement, and sustain WASH services, in line with localization and sustainable solutions

Theme: Coordination, partnerships, integration, and operational planning
Competency activity: Apply knowledge of key concepts of market-based program and private sector engagement (e.g., understanding local supply chains, service providers, and feasibility of market-based options) and engage the private sector appropriately

Theme: Coordination, partnerships, integration, and operational planning
Competency activity: Demonstrate ability to integrate and apply context analysis to inform strategic planning, sequencing and integration of WASH response options including nexus thinking within the Humanitarian-Development-Peace (HDP) context, ensuring that Humanitarian WASH interventions are coordinated and complementary to Peace and Development interventions, and contribute to long-term stability and resilience

Theme: Community engagement, accountability and user-centered programming

Theme: Community engagement, accountability and user-centered programming
Competency activity: Facilitate meaningful and inclusive participation of affected populations in program design and implementation

Theme: Community engagement, accountability and user-centered programming

Competency activity: Provide clear, accurate, and accessible information to communities about WASH services, project decisions, activities, plans, entitlements, and available support, including communicating how community feedback has been considered and used

Theme: Community engagement, accountability and user-centered programming
Competency activity: Establish and support safe, accessible, and appropriate community feedback and complaint mechanisms

Theme: Community engagement, accountability and user-centered programming
Competency activity: Collect, document, and share community feedback with program teams in a timely manner

Theme: Community engagement, accountability and user-centered programming
Competency activity: Use community feedback and monitoring information to improve program quality and effectiveness
Apply protection, gender, and inclusion principles relevant to WASH programming and service delivery across the project cycle
Identify and mitigate protection risks linked to WASH services and activities (e.g., safety, privacy, dignity, GBV risks), and escalate concerns appropriately
Promote equitable access to WASH services for underrepresented groups and monitor barriers to access
Adapt WASH activities and communication approaches for different literacy levels, languages, and cultural contexts

Theme: Digital literacy, data management, and data responsibility

Theme: Digital literacy, data management, and data responsibility
Competency activity: Select and use appropriate paper-based or digital tools (e.g., Word, Excel, Kobo/ODK, Google Earth, mWater), based on context, capacity, connectivity, and constraints

Competency activity: Collect, manage, or review WASH data using digital or paper-based tools in a way that is accurate, complete, and timely

Theme: Digital literacy, data management, and data responsibility
Competency activity: Use mapping, dashboards, or visualizations (e.g., Google Earth, mWater, QGIS outputs) to analyze and inform decisions and communicate key WASH findings to relevant audiences
Assess capacity strengthening needs of staff, volunteers, partners, or community promoters using appropriate tools and methods, and prioritize gaps in relation to program objectives
Design capacity strengthening plans and training sessions that are tailored to the learning needs, literacy levels, languages, and prior experience of the target audience
Develop and adapt training materials and job aids that are practical, accessible, and appropriate for the context and audience
Facilitate training sessions, workshops, and community learning activities using participatory and adult learning approaches
Provide follow-up coaching, mentoring, and on-the-job support to staff, volunteers, service providers, and community promoters to reinforce learning and improve practice
Demonstrate knowledge of key concepts of urban WASH, including engagement with municipal and private sector service providers (see Urban Competency Framework, RedR UK)
Demonstrate knowledge of WASH in healthcare facilities in support of infection prevention and control (IPC), including use of relevant assessment tools (e.g., WASH FIT, WASH FACET)

Demonstrate the ability to design, implement, and monitor WASH interventions tailored to the specific needs and challenges of rural communities

Competency activity: Pre-test hygiene promotion materials with target audiences and adapt based on feedback

conduct environmental risk assessment including assessing open burning practice and pollution to soil, air and water (surface and groundwater) to inform mitigation strategies for SWM system design/improvement
